heaviest_rainfall.status: 47.89mm of rain was recorded as having fallen on 09 November 2025, marking an exceptional 144.8 hour precipitation event.  At times the rain was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain was recorded on 02 September 2025 with a peak rate of 34.3mm per hour.  The storm lasted 15.0 hours and had a total rainfall of 21.19mm.

longest_rain.status: On 09 November 2025, the rain lasted for 144.8 hours, which is the longest time recorded by our database.  47.89mm accumulated and it fell at 24.6mm per hour at times.

most_lightning.status: Since records began on 11 April 2025, the highest lightning strike count in one day is 225 on 28 May 2026.

storm.status: No storms are expected.  The last storm was observed on 27 May 2026 and continued for 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is no cold snap at the moment.  This year has experienced 3 cold snaps, with an average of 3.7 per year.

extreme.status: The record temperature difference stands at 27.7°C (-4.5 - 23.2°C), set on 24 April 2026.

heatwaves.status: At the moment, it is not a heatwaves.  This year has seen 1 heatwaves; typically, 1.8 heatwaves happen every year.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was on 18 July 2025 and it has only happened 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (228°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The record wind gust remains at 128.2 mph from 27 January 2026.

run.status: The top recorded wind run is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The top recorded wind speed is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 5.7°C), outside temperature (down 4.1°C), and inside temperature (down 1.5°C) and drier conditions in rainfall (down 0.2 mm).

The past week and month had observable variation in feels like temperature (up 5.0°C), dew point temperature (up 2.7°C), outside temperature (up 3.1°C), and cloud levels (up 197 m) when compared to today.

Today’s average cloud levels is 111.0m higher than yesterday, 196.8m lower than the past week, 455.4m higher than the past month.

Yesterday, cloud levels hit a low of 1381m and a high of 5070m, averaging 3100m; the past week and month reported somewhat higher averages.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures will remain comfortably hot. Outside; the temperature will feel pleasantly warm. Also, neither too dry nor too humid and little or no wind. Showers, mostly clear skies with high cloud, and sunlight will be very intense and bright. Little change in the weather.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
